title: Lenovo Y700(Gen 5) debuts with SD 8 Gen Elite 5, Xiaoxin Pro 13 and Pro GT 13 also unveiled
description: Lenovo announced a trio of new Android tablets in China with some impressive specs. The Lenovo Y700 (Gen 5) is one of the first tablets to launch with Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset. This is actually the same device as the Lenovo Legion Tab, which we got to spend time with at MWC. Alongside the Y700 (Gen 5), Lenovo also unveiled its Xiaoxin Pro 13 and Pro GT 13 slates, which bring high-res 13-inch IPS LCDs, big batteries and top-tier Snapdragon chipsets. Lenovo Legion Y700 (Gen 5) Legion Y700 (Gen 5) brings a compact 8.8-inch IPS LCD panel with 3,040 x 1,904px...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold 2 and Galaxy Z Slide are on the way, first details leak
description: Samsung has just discontinued the Galaxy Z TriFold, its first double-folding smartphone, in Korea, after a very short three-month run. However, the company is apparently not done with the form factor entirely. [#InlinePriceWidget,14292,1#] Instead, it's already developing the TriFold's successor, which we'll call the TriFold 2 despite how hilariously dumb that sounds. Anyway, according to a source in Korea, the TriFold 2 will arrive in mid-2027. Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold It will be thinner and lighter than the original TriFold. In fact, the TriFold 2 is said to be only very...

title: Huawei nova 16 Ultra details leak
description: Huawei launched the nova 15 Ultra a few months ago, and now it's allegedly already hard at work on its successor, which will presumably be called nova 16 Ultra. According to a new rumor from China, the Huawei nova 16 Ultra will be powered by a flagship Kirin 9 series SoC. The phone will keep the same 6.84-inch LTPO OLED screen of its predecessor, but will have a bigger, 7,000 mAh battery. Huawei nova 15 Ultra The main camera will have the same 50MP resolution but will get a new 1/1.3-inch type sensor. The 50MP periscope telephoto camera will stay unchanged compared to the nova 15...

title: Apple iPhone 17e review
Honor Magic9 to pack a camera punch in a small package
description: According to the prolific Chinese leaker who goes by Digital Chat Station on Weibo, Honor is currently working on a new smartphone with a 6.36-inch flat screen and an 8,000 mAh battery. This may in fact be the Magic9 the same source talked about earlier this month, or a different handset altogether, it's still unclear. Anyway, let's call it the Magic9 for now. It will allegedly have a 200MP main camera, using a 1/1.28-inch type sensor, a 50MP ultrawide, and a 64MP periscope telephoto camera using a 1/2-inch type sensor. Honor Magic8 The phone won't have 3D face recognition, which...

title: Oppo A6x launches in India as the A6s, here are the specs and prices
description: Just as was hinted at by a recent leak, today the Oppo A6x became official in India, where it's called A6s, despite the fact that there's also an international A6s with a different battery capacity. Anyway, the Indian A6s comes with a 6.75-inch 720x1570 LCD touchscreen with 120Hz refresh rate, 240Hz touch sampling rate, and 1,125-nit peak brightness, the MediaTek Dimensity 6300 SoC at the helm, a 50MP rear camera, a 2MP decorative monochrome camera, a 5MP front-facing camera, and a 6,500 mAh battery with support for 45W wired charging. Oppo A6s (India) official images You can...

title: Here's when the OnePlus Nord Buds 4 Pro go on sale and how much they cost
description: The OnePlus Nord Buds 4 Pro, the successors to the Nord Buds 3 Pro from 2024, are officially launching tomorrow. Ahead of that, Amazon has today revealed the date of their first sale, as well as their price. The Nord Buds 4 Pro will first be up for grabs on March 23, at 12 PM local time in India, according to the retailer. And you will get an INR 200 price reduction on that day, from the Buds 4 Pro's normal price of INR 3,999 down to INR 3,799. The Nord Buds 4 Pro have 12mm titanium-coated drivers, support for the LHDC codec, 55dB ANC, AI-enhanced call clarity with six mics, 3D...

title: AMD buys HBM4 from Samsung for AI data centers
description: AMD is buying Samsung's HBM4 high-bandwidth memory for its upcoming "AI accelerator", the Instinct MI455X GPU, as well as 6th gen AMD Epyc CPUs codenamed Venice. AI data center operators can combine Instinct GPUs with Epyc CPUs and rack-scale architectures such as the AMD Helios platform to support next-gen AI systems. The memorandum of understanding between Samsung and AMD on these matters was announced today at Samsung's most advanced chip manufacturing complex in Pyeongtaek, Korea. The signing ceremony was attended by AMD CEO Dr. Lisa Su and Samsung Electronics CEO and Vice Chairman...

title: OnePlus Nord 6 leak brings info about RAM, storage, and pricing
description: A couple of days ago, OnePlus started teasing an upcoming Nord phone in India, and we assume this is the Nord 6. Amazon India has also launched its very own teaser microsite for the device, which obviously reveals the fact that it will be sold by Amazon. Today a tipster over on X tells us that the Nord 6 will arrive in India in two RAM/storage iterations: 8/256GB and 12/256GB. Interestingly enough, there won't be a 512GB option, nor a 128GB one. The same tipster claims that the Nord 6 won't be launching alongside a Nord CE6 - the latter isn't planned "for now". It's unclear if it...

title: Realme C100i emerges in several listings ahead of launch
description: A yet-to-be-released Realme C-series smartphone leaked across several Amazon listings as well as Thailand’s NBTC certification database. The Realme C100i was spotted in premature listings across Amazon Spain, Australia, Italy and France. Realme C100i listing on Amazon These listings also revealed some of the phone’s key specs, though they vary across the different storefronts, which implies these may not be accurate. Either way, the C100i is expected to be a 4G device featuring a ‎6.8-inch LCD, a 6,000mAh battery and 4/64GB and 4/128GB trims. It’s also expected to bring a 50MP...

title: Oppo Find X9s Pro supposedly gets certified in China
description: Oppo has long been rumored to plan on launching the Find X9s alongside the Find X9 Ultra in the coming weeks, and we've already heard plenty of things about the X9s - including the fact that it will be available internationally. Now, a tipster over on X claims the Find X9s Pro has been certified in China by 3C with the model number PME110. If this is actually the Find X9s Pro, then it might be China-only. Anyway, its certification has revealed support for 80W wired charging and not much else. If there is going to be a Find X9s Pro, then surely it will be unveiled at the same event...

title: Huawei Enjoy 90 Pro Max color options revealed ahead of launch
description: The Huawei Enjoy 90 Pro Max and Enjoy 90 Plus are set to launch in China next week. Ahead of the debut, Huawei has revealed the full range of color options for the Pro Max variant. In a post on Weibo, Huawei confirmed that the phone will be available in Feitian Green, Morning Gold, Obsidian Black, and Snow White color options. The color options closely mirror those offered on Huawei’s Mate 80 Pro flagship. Notably, the company has yet to reveal detailed specifications of the upcoming device. That said, a recent teaser suggests the Enjoy 90 Pro Max will feature a dual...

title: iPhone 17e rear panel adds MagSafe to iPhone 16e, teardown reveals
description: Earlier this month, Apple introduced the new iPhone 17e with modest upgrades over the iPhone 16e, including the addition of MagSafe. Now, a teardown of the device suggests that iPhone 16e users may also be able to gain MagSafe functionality through a relatively simple hardware upgrade. The team at iFixit performed the teardown and found that replacing the iPhone 16e’s rear panel with that of the newer iPhone 17e brings MagSafe support to the older model. iPhone 16e (left) and iPhone 17e (right) However, the modification comes with a few limitations. For one, the iPhone 16e lacks...

title: OnePlus 15T launch set for next week, pre-orders open
description: Several teasers later, OnePlus has finally set a launch date for the 15T. Alongside, the company has also opened pre-orders for the OnePlus 15T in China. The OnePlus 15T will be unveiled in China on March 24. It will be offered in Healing White Chocolate, Pure Cocoa, and Relaxing Matcha color options. Interested customers can also pre-order the handset in China via the official Oppo online store. It will be sold in 12GB/256GB, 12GB/512GB, 16GB/256GB, 16GB/512GB, and 16GB/1TB variants. Ahead of the launch, OnePlus has already confirmed several key specifications of the 15T. It...

title: Nothing Phone (4a) series receiving new update with these features
description: Nothing has announced the rollout of a new update for the recently launched Phone (4a) and Phone (4a) Pro. The Nothing OS update introduces several new features, including enhancements to Live Updates on the Glyph Bar and a new Relaxation Hub, among others. Starting with Android 16’s Live Updates, apps that support real-time updates will now use the Glyph Bar of the Nothing Phone (4a) to show the progress. The feature works across the always-on display, lock screen, status bar, and notifications. Additionally, a new Relaxation Hub has been introduced, featuring Breathing Break...

title: Xiaomi 17T nabs another certification on its way to a rumored early launch
description: The Xiaomi T series devices have always launched in September, but that may change this year. According to a recent rumor, the 17T and 17T Pro may get official as early as May, and that theory does seem to be supported by the fact that they've already been certified by a bunch of authorities across the globe. Today, the Xiaomi 17T was discovered in Singapore's IMDA database, following its certification by BIS in India. The phone has the model number 2602DPT53G globally, and 2602DPT53I in India. IMDA has revealed that it supports wireless charging, unlike its predecessor from last...

title: Rumor: iPhone 19e may bring ProMotion display
description: It’s been less than a week since the iPhone 17e was announced, but we’re already getting our first batch of rumors for its successor’s successor. The iPhone 19e, which should launch in early 2028, is now rumored to bring a ProMotion display. That’s a variable refresh rate panel in Apple lingo, which would be a breakthrough for the budget “e” lineup, which has thus been limited to 60Hz panels. According to a new report from ZDNet Korea, Apple is currently developing a new LTPO+ technology which uses oxide materials for both switching and driving transistors, leading to improved power...

title: Samsung Galaxy S26 FE, M47 and F70 Pro emerge in GSMA listings
description: A trio of upcoming Samsung phones appeared on the GSMA database. The Galaxy S26 FE (SM-S741B/DS), Galaxy M47 5G (SM-M476B/DS) and Galaxy F70 Pro 5G (SM-E476B/DS) were spotted in certification listings confirming their existence. Galaxy S26 FE (SM-S741B/DS) GSMA listing The S26 Fan Edition could very well be the most anticipated device out of the bunch since it will be the fourth member of the flagship S26 series. Apart from its model number and naming, the listing does not include any specs or images of the device. Samsung launched its last two Galaxy S-series FE phones in...

title: Oppo K14's launch was delayed, here's when the first sale is taking place
description: The Oppo K14 was supposed to launch in India on March 9, but that got delayed for whatever reason. Today, the brand has finally revealed when the first sale will take place: on March 20. The phone is priced at INR 16,999 and will be offered through Flipkart. The K14 has a 120Hz LCD screen with 1,125-nit peak brightness, and it's powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 6300 SoC, paired with 8GB of RAM and 256GB of storage. It has a 50MP main camera, and a 7,000 mAh battery with support for 45W wired charging. There's an IP69 rating for dust and water resistance, and a promise of 60-month...

title: Samsung starts buying Chinese display panels for its mid-range smartphones
description: According to a new report from Korea, Samsung is diversifying its display supply chain. This creates a funny situation where Samsung (Electronics) will stop buying screens from Samsung (Display) for some mid-range devices, instead opting to purchase from CSOT, owned by TCL. CSOT's panels are at least 20% cheaper than Samsung's, and the move is speculated to be aimed at offsetting rising memory costs by lowering other device costs in the mid-range. Samsung has allegedly ordered 15 million display panels from CSOT, which will be used in the upcoming Galaxy A57 as well as some unnamed...
